**IMPORTANT: Before using any chrome browser tools, you MUST first load them using ToolSearch.**

Chrome browser tools are MCP tools that require loading before use. Before calling any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tool:

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching.
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- pfw-testing: Write tests to assert against how you expect your Swift applications to work. Includes techniques for base `@Suite`s and `@Test` traits, dependency control, better test failures.
- pfw-dependencies: Control your app's dependencies to make its logic easier to test and preview using the Dependencies library
- pfw-observable-models: Move Swift logic and behavior out of the UI and into a class that is easier to understand in isolation and test.
- pfw-custom-dump: Pretty-print values (`customDump`), diff values (`diff`), and assert against values (`expectDifference`, and `expectNoDifference`) using the CustomDump library.
- pfw-macro-testing: Snapshot test your Swift macros using the MacroTesting library
- pfw-case-paths: Add ergonomics and generic algorithms to Swift enums using key paths and the CasePaths library.
- pfw-snapshot-testing: Snapshot test your Swift code using the SnapshotTesting library
- pfw-composable-architecture: Build Swift application logic out of declarative units using the Composable Architecture (TCA) library.
- pfw-modern-swiftui: Build complex SwiftUI views using the modern, best practices: naming conventions, correct API usage (bindings, state initialization), powerful tips and tricks.
- pfw-structured-queries: Write type-safe, schema-safe SQL using the StructuredQueries library
- pfw-perception: Back-port Swift Observation to earlier platforms using the Perception library
- pfw-issue-reporting: Write ergonomic, unobtrusive error handling using the IssueReporting library and the `reportIssue` and `withErrorReporting` functions.
- pfw-sharing: Share and persist values across your application with the Sharing library
- pfw-spm: Make changes to the Package.swift of an SPM package, such as adding targets, libraries, dependencies, macros, test targets, and integrate with Xcode projects and workspaces.
- pfw-pfw: Improve the use of any skill whose name is prefixed with "pfw-".
- pfw-identified-collections: Performant, ergonomic access to a Swift collection's elements using the IdentifiedCollections library
- pfw-sqlite-data: Use the SQLiteData library to read and write to a SQLite database, observe queries (`@FetchAll`, `@FetchOne`, `@Fetch`), synchronize to iCloud (`SyncEngine`), and share records with other iCloud users.
- pfw-swift-navigation: Implement advanced state-driven navigation patterns in Swift apps, including enum domain modeling using the SwiftNavigation package.
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.description: Explore user settings and dashboard
input.prompt:
I need to understand how user settings work in this Rails app and how the sandbox dashboard displays sandbox cards. Specifically:

1. How does the User model handle settings/preferences? Look for any existing settings fields, JSON columns, or settings patterns.
2. How does the dashboard display sandbox cards? Find the dashboard view templates and controllers.
3. What data is available per sandbox (hostname, port, username, etc.)?
4. Are there any existing "link" or "action" buttons on sandbox cards?

Search thoroughly in:
- app/models/user.rb
- app/controllers/ (dashboard, sandboxes controllers)
- app/views/ (dashboard views, sandbox partials)
- db/schema.rb or migrations for the user table schema
- Any existing settings/preferences patterns

Report file paths, key methods, and the structure of sandbox card rendering.

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary based on all the information I've gathered:

## Comprehensive Analysis: User Settings and Sandbox Card Display

### 1. User Model Settings/Preferences

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/user.rb`

The User model stores settings as individual database columns (not a JSON store). Key settings fields:

- **Boolean settings:**
  - `admin` - Admin flag
  - `tailscale_auto_connect` - Auto-connect to Tailscale
  - `chrome_persist_profile` - Persist Chrome profile
  - `must_change_password` - Force password change on next login

- **String/Text settings:**
  - `smb_password` - SMB password (encrypted)
  - `ssh_public_key` - SSH public key
  - `terminal_emulator` - Terminal type (`"xterm"` or `"ghostty"`)
  - `tailscale_auth_key` - Tailscale authentication
  - `tailscale_state` - Tailscale status (`"disabled"`, `"pending"`, `"enabled"`)
  - `tailscale_network`, `tailscale_subnet`, `tailscale_container_id` - Tailscale config

- **Integer settings:**
  - `sandbox_archive_retention_days` - Archives retention (nullable, falls back to `Setting.instance.sandbox_archive_retention_days` or 30)

**User methods:**
- `tailscale_enabled?` - Check if Tailscale is enabled
- `tailscale_pending?` - Check if Tailscale setup is pending
- `tailscale_disabled?` - Check if Tailscale is disabled
- `effective_archive_retention_days` - Returns user's setting or global setting (line 63-67)

### 2. Global Settings Model

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/setting.rb`

Singleton pattern (`Setting.instance`). Stores system-wide defaults:
- `sandbox_archive_retention_days` (default: 30)
- `default_vnc_enabled`, `default_mount_home`, `default_docker_enabled`
- `default_data_path`
- OAuth configs (GitHub, Google)
- SMTP settings

### 3. Dashboard Controller & Sandbox Card Display

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/controllers/dashboard_controller.rb`

**`index` action (lines 2-7):**
- Loads all sandboxes with user and routes: `@sandboxes = policy_scope(Sandbox).includes(:user, :routes).order(:name)`
- Loads archived sandboxes: `@archived_sandboxes = Current.user.sandboxes.archived.includes(:routes).order(:name)`
- Computes active VNC IDs from filesystem: `@vnc_active_ids = running_vnc_ids`
- Fetches Tailscale IPs for each sandbox: `@tailscale_ips = tailscale_ips_for(@sandboxes)`

**`card` action (lines 9-18):**
- Updates individual sandbox card via Turbo Stream
- Renders partial: `"dashboard/sandbox"` with `sandbox`, `vnc_active`, `tailscale_ip` locals
- Handles missing sandboxes gracefully

### 4. Sandbox Card Rendering

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/dashboard/_sandbox.html.erb` (18KB template)

**Data available per sandbox:**
```
Sandbox model fields:
- id, name, status (pending/running/stopped/destroyed/archived)
- container_id, image, image_version
- user_id, created_at, updated_at
- job_status, job_error, job_started_at (for tracking operations)
- mount_home, data_path (persistent data)
- tailscale (boolean), smb_enabled, temporary
- vnc_enabled, vnc_geometry, vnc_depth
- docker_enabled, persistent_volume
- ssh_port
- routes (association) - each has: domain, port, mode (http/tcp), public_port, url

Associated via user:
- user.name, user.smb_password, user.tailscale_*
- Local vars: vnc_active (boolean), tailscale_ip (string IP)
```

**Action Buttons on Sandbox Cards (lines 100-179):**

For **running** sandboxes:
- `terminal_sandbox_path(sandbox, type: "tmux")` - Open tmux terminal
- `terminal_sandbox_path(sandbox, type: "shell")` - Open shell terminal
- `vnc_sandbox_path(sandbox)` - Open VNC desktop (if `vnc_enabled?`)
- Snapshot button (opens modal, posts to `snapshot_sandbox_path`)
- `logs_sandbox_path(sandbox)` - View logs
- `stop_sandbox_path(sandbox)` - Stop sandbox

For **stopped** sandboxes:
- `start_sandbox_path(sandbox)` - Start sandbox
- `logs_sandbox_path(sandbox)` - View logs (if container exists)

For **failed jobs**:
- `retry_sandbox_path(sandbox)` - Retry operation

Always available:
- `sandbox_path(sandbox)` - Edit sandbox (delete link)
- "Destroy" button with confirmation dialog

**Pills/Feature Tags on Card (lines 49-97):**
- TEMP badge (if temporary)
- HOME badge - static or clickable SMB link if `mount_home?` and Tailscale IP available
- PERSISTED badge - static or clickable SMB link if data mounted and Tailscale IP available
- Tailscale badge (shows Tailscale IP if available)
- Route badges - clickable links to `route.url` (https://domain:port)

### 5. Sandbox Model Key Methods

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/sandbox.rb`

- `job_in_progress?` - Returns true if `job_status` is present
- `job_failed?` - Returns true if `job_error` is present
- `start_job(status)` - Sets job_status, clears job_error
- `finish_job()` - Clears job fields
- `fail_job(error_message)` - Sets job_error, clears status

**Turbo Broadcasts:**
- `broadcast_prepend_to_dashboard` - New sandbox added (uses partial `dashboard/sandbox`)
- `broadcast_replace_to_dashboard` - Sandbox updated
- `broadcast_remove_from_dashboard` - Sandbox deleted/archived

### 6. Routes and Paths

**File:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/config/routes.rb`

Sandbox routes (lines 32-51):
```
POST   /sandboxes/:id/start          -> start_sandbox_path
POST   /sandboxes/:id/stop           -> stop_sandbox_path
POST   /sandboxes/:id/rebuild        -> rebuild_sandbox_path
POST   /sandboxes/:id/retry          -> retry_sandbox_path
DELETE /sandboxes/:id                -> sandbox_path (delete)
POST   /sandboxes/:id/terminal       -> terminal_sandbox_path
POST   /sandboxes/:id/vnc            -> vnc_sandbox_path
POST   /sandboxes/:id/snapshot       -> snapshot_sandbox_path
GET    /sandboxes/:id/logs           -> logs_sandbox_path
GET    /sandboxes/:id/card           -> card_sandbox_path (dashboard.card action)
GET    /sandboxes/:id/stats          -> stats_sandbox_path
GET    /sandboxes/:id/metrics        -> metrics_sandbox_path
POST   /sandboxes/:id/archive_restore -> archive_restore_sandbox_path
DELETE /sandboxes/:id/purge          -> purge_sandbox_path
```

### 7. Dashboard View Structure

**Files:**
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/dashboard/index.html.erb` - Main dashboard (subscribes to Turbo Stream)
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/dashboard/_sandbox.html.erb` - Active sandbox card
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/dashboard/_archived_sandbox.html.erb` - Archived sandbox card
- `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/dashboard/_sandbox_stats.html.erb` - Stats partial

The dashboard uses:
- Turbo Streams for real-time updates: `turbo_stream_from [Current.user, "dashboard"]`
- Stimulus controllers for polling: `sandbox-polling` and `sparkline`
- Modal for snapshot creation (inline per card)

class User < ApplicationRecord
  has_secure_password
  encrypts :smb_password

  def smb_password
    super
  rescue ActiveRecord::Encryption::Errors::Decryption
    nil
  end
  has_many :sessions, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :sandboxes, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :api_tokens, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :oauth_identities, dependent: :destroy

  normalizes :email_address, with: ->(e) { e.strip.downcase }
  normalizes :name, with: ->(n) { n.strip.downcase }

  validates :name, presence: true, uniqueness: true,
    format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{1,30}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric (2-31 chars, start with letter)" }
  validates :email_address, presence: true, uniqueness: true
  validates :status, inclusion: { in: %w[active suspended pending_approval] }
  validates :sandbox_archive_retention_days, numericality: { only_integer: true, greater_than_or_equal_to: 0, allow_nil: true }
  validates :terminal_emulator, inclusion: { in: %w[ghostty xterm], allow_nil: true }

  scope :active, -> { where(status: "active") }

  def admin?
    admin
  end

  def active?
    status == "active"
  end

  def suspended?
    status == "suspended"
  end

  def pending_approval?
    status == "pending_approval"
  end

  def tailscale_enabled?
    respond_to?(:tailscale_state) && tailscale_state == "enabled"
  end

  def tailscale_pending?
    respond_to?(:tailscale_state) && tailscale_state == "pending"
  end

  def tailscale_disabled?
    !respond_to?(:tailscale_state) || tailscale_state == "disabled"
  end

  def tailscale_auto_connect?
    tailscale_auto_connect
  end

  def chrome_persist_profile?
    chrome_persist_profile
  end

  def effective_archive_retention_days
    return sandbox_archive_retention_days if sandbox_archive_retention_days.present?

    @effective_archive_retention_days_fallback ||= Setting.instance.sandbox_archive_retention_days || 30
  end
end

    t.datetime "expires_at", null: false
    t.string "status", default: "pending", null: false
    t.datetime "updated_at", null: false
    t.string "user_code", null: false
    t.integer "user_id"
    t.index ["api_token_id"], name: "index_device_codes_on_api_token_id"
    t.index ["code"], name: "index_device_codes_on_code", unique: true
    t.index ["user_code"], name: "index_device_codes_on_user_code"
    t.index ["user_id"], name: "index_device_codes_on_user_id"

    t.datetime "updated_at", null: false

  end

  create_table "users", force: :cascade do |t|

    t.datetime "created_at", null: false
    t.string "email_address", null: false
    t.string "full_name"

    t.string "network_name"
    t.string "network_subnet"
    t.string "password_digest", null: false
    t.integer "sandbox_archive_retention_days"
    t.text "smb_password"
    t.text "ssh_public_key"
    t.string "status", default: "active", null: false
    t.string "tailscale_auth_key"

    t.string "tailscale_container_id"
    t.string "tailscale_network"
    t.string "tailscale_state", default: "disabled", null: false
    t.string "tailscale_subnet"

    t.index ["email_address"], name: "index_users_on_email_address", unique: true

  end

  add_foreign_key "api_tokens", "users"

input.prompt:
Find these specific things in the Sandcastle Rails app:

1. The user settings/profile edit page - the controller and view where users can edit their settings (terminal_emulator, tailscale_auto_connect, etc.)
2. How does the sandbox hostname get determined? Look for SANDCASTLE_HOST env var usage, and how SSH connection strings are built (e.g. what hostname/port would a user SSH to)
3. The users controller - specifically the update action and what params are permitted

Search in:
- app/controllers/users_controller.rb or app/controllers/profiles_controller.rb or similar
- app/views/users/ or app/views/profiles/ or app/views/settings/
- Any helper that builds SSH URLs or connection info
- Look for SANDCASTLE_HOST references

Report file paths, line numbers, and relevant code snippets.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings:

## Summary of Findings

I've located all the key components you requested in the Sandcastle Rails app:

### 1. User Settings/Profile Edit Page

**Controller:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/controllers/settings_controller.rb`

The SettingsController handles user settings with:
- **Line 7-14:** `update_profile` action that updates user profile with `profile_params`
- **Line 75-77:** `profile_params` method that permits these fields:
  - `email_address`
  - `full_name`
  - `github_username`
  - `chrome_persist_profile`
  - `sandbox_archive_retention_days`
  - `terminal_emulator` (ghostty or xterm)

**Also includes:**
- **Line 43-52:** `toggle_tailscale` action for toggling `tailscale_auto_connect` setting
- **Line 32-41:** `update_smb_password` action for setting SMB password

**View:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/settings/show.html.erb`

The settings page includes sections for:
- Profile (Line 18-55): Full name, email, GitHub username
- Terminal emulator selection (Line 153-170): Choose between Ghostty Web and xterm.js
- Tailscale (Line 83-132): Status display and auto-connect toggle
- VNC (Line 134-151): Chrome profile persistence
- SMB/File Sharing (Line 261-293): SMB password configuration

---

### 2. Sandbox Hostname Determination (SANDCASTLE_HOST)

**SANDCASTLE_HOST Usage Locations:**

1. **Guide View:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/pages/guide.html.erb:7`
   - Fetches: `host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "sandcastle.example.com")`
   - Used to display login and route setup instructions

2. **Terminal Manager:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/services/terminal_manager.rb:58`
   - Fetches: `host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")`
   - Used in Traefik configuration for terminal URL routing (Lines 62-68)

3. **VNC Manager:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/services/vnc_manager.rb`
   - Referenced for VNC hostname configuration

4. **Route Manager:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/services/route_manager.rb:185, 220`
   - Line 185: `host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")`
   - Line 220: `host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")`
   - Used for SSL certificate generation

5. **Production Config:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/config/environments/production.rb`
   - Action Cable URL, allowed hosts, and mailer config use SANDCASTLE_HOST

**SSH Connection Information:**

The app uses **Tailscale IPs for SSH connections** rather than traditional SSH ports. Users don't directly SSH to a hostname/port combination. Instead:

- Each sandbox gets a **Tailscale IP** when `tailscale: true` is set
- Users access the sandbox via the CLI using `sandcastle connect <name>` or `sandcastle ssh <name>`
- The Tailscale IP is shown on the sandbox detail page (displayed in dashboard pill - see `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/views/dashboard/_sandbox.html.erb:89`)
- For SMB file sharing, the connection uses the **Tailscale IP on port 445**: `smb://<tailscale-ip>/home` (Lines 144-150 of show.html.erb)

---

### 3. Users Controller - Update Action and Permitted Params

**API Users Controller:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/controllers/api/users_controller.rb`

- **Line 20-24:** `update` action calls `@user.update!(user_params)`
- **Line 41-43:** `user_params` method that permits:
  - `name`
  - `full_name`
  - `github_username`
  - `email_address`
  - `password`
  - `password_confirmation`
  - `ssh_public_key`
  - `admin`
  - `status`

**Admin Users Controller:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/controllers/admin/users_controller.rb`

- **Line 29-35:** `update` action for admin panel
- **Line 53-55:** `user_params` using `params.expect(user: [ ... ])` with same permitted fields as API controller

**User Model:** `/Users/thies/Projects/GitHub/Sandcastle/app/models/user.rb`

- Lines 43-57: Tailscale-related methods (`tailscale_enabled?`, `tailscale_pending?`, `tailscale_disabled?`, `tailscale_auto_connect?`)
- The model stores: `ssh_public_key`, `smb_password` (encrypted), `tailscale_auto_connect`, `terminal_emulator`, `sandbox_archive_retention_days`, etc.

file.content:
class SettingsController < ApplicationController
  def show
    @user = Current.user
    @api_tokens = @user.api_tokens.active.order(created_at: :desc)
  end

  def update_profile
    @user = Current.user

    if @user.update(profile_params)
      redirect_to settings_path, notice: "Profile updated successfully."
    else
      render :show, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def update_password
    @user = Current.user

    unless @user.authenticate(params[:current_password])
      redirect_to settings_path, alert: "Current password is incorrect."
      return
    end

    if @user.update(password_params)
      redirect_to settings_path, notice: "Password changed successfully."
    else
      redirect_to settings_path, alert: @user.errors.full_messages.join(", ")
    end
  end

  def update_smb_password
    @user = Current.user

    if @user.update(smb_password_params)
      SandboxManager.new.update_smb_password(user: @user)
      redirect_to settings_path, notice: "SMB password updated."
    else
      redirect_to settings_path, alert: @user.errors.full_messages.join(", ").presence || "Failed to update SMB password."
    end
  end

  def toggle_tailscale
    @user = Current.user

    if @user.update(tailscale_auto_connect: !@user.tailscale_auto_connect)
      status = @user.tailscale_auto_connect ? "enabled" : "disabled"
      redirect_to settings_path, notice: "Tailscale auto-connect #{status}."
    else
      redirect_to settings_path, alert: "Failed to update Tailscale settings."
    end
  end

  def generate_token
    @user = Current.user

    token_name = params[:name].presence || "Web UI Token"
    token, raw_token = ApiToken.generate_for(@user, name: token_name)

    flash[:api_token] = raw_token
    redirect_to settings_path, notice: "API token '#{token_name}' generated. Make sure to copy it now - you won't be able to see it again!"
  end

  def revoke_token
    @user = Current.user
    token = @user.api_tokens.find(params[:id])
    token_name = token.name
    token.destroy!

    redirect_to settings_path, notice: "API token '#{token_name}' revoked."
  end

  private

  def profile_params
    params.require(:user).permit(:email_address, :full_name, :github_username, :chrome_persist_profile, :sandbox_archive_retention_days, :terminal_emulator)
  end

  def password_params
    params.require(:user).permit(:password, :password_confirmation)
  end

  def smb_password_params
    params.require(:user).permit(:smb_password)
  end
end

Rails.application.routes.draw do
  root "dashboard#index"

  # Mount Action Cable for WebSocket connections
  mount ActionCable.server => "/cable"

  resource :session
  resources :passwords, param: :token

  get  "invites/:token",        to: "registrations#new",    as: :new_registration
  post "invites/:token/accept", to: "registrations#create", as: :accept_registration

  get  "auth/:provider/callback", to: "oauth_callbacks#create"
  post "auth/:provider/callback", to: "oauth_callbacks#create"
  get  "auth/failure",            to: "oauth_callbacks#failure"
  resource :change_password, only: [ :show, :update ]

  resource :settings, only: :show do
    patch :update_profile
    patch :update_password

    end
    resources :snapshots, only: [ :index, :create, :show, :destroy ], param: :name
    resources :users
    resource :status, only: :show, controller: "status"
    resource :info, only: :show
    resources :tokens, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ]
    namespace :auth do
      post :device_code

    end
    resource :tailscale, only: [], controller: "tailscale" do

      get :login_status

    end
    resource :smb, only: [], controller: "smb" do
      patch :set_password
    end
  end

  get "guide", to: "pages#guide"

  get "up" => "rails/health#show", as: :rails_health_check
end

file.content:
class DashboardController < ApplicationController
  def index
    @sandboxes = policy_scope(Sandbox).includes(:user, :routes).order(:name)
    @archived_sandboxes = Current.user.sandboxes.archived.includes(:routes).order(:name)
    @vnc_active_ids = running_vnc_ids
    @tailscale_ips = tailscale_ips_for(@sandboxes)
  end

  def card
    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).includes(:user, :routes).find(params[:id])
    authorize sandbox
    vnc_active = VncManager.new.active?(sandbox: sandbox)
    tailscale_ip = sandbox.tailscale? ? TailscaleManager.new.sandbox_tailscale_ip(sandbox: sandbox) : nil
    render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.replace(helpers.dom_id(sandbox), partial: "dashboard/sandbox", locals: { sandbox: sandbox, vnc_active: vnc_active, tailscale_ip: tailscale_ip })
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    # Sandbox was destroyed/archived while the dashboard was open — remove the card
    render turbo_stream: turbo_stream.remove("sandbox_#{params[:id]}")
  end

  def stats
    sandbox = policy_scope(Sandbox).find(params[:id])
    authorize sandbox, :stats?

    if sandbox.status == "running" && sandbox.container_id.present?
      @stats = Rails.cache.fetch("sandbox_stats_full:#{sandbox.id}", expires_in: 4.seconds) do
        container = Docker::Container.get(sandbox.container_id)
        raw = container.stats(stream: false)

        next nil if raw.blank?

        networks = raw["networks"] || {}
        net_rx = networks.values.sum { |n| n["rx_bytes"] || 0 }
        net_tx = networks.values.sum { |n| n["tx_bytes"] || 0 }

        blkio = raw.dig("blkio_stats", "io_service_bytes_recursive") || []
        disk_read = blkio.select { |e| e["op"]&.downcase == "read" }.sum { |e| e["value"] || 0 }
        disk_write = blkio.select { |e| e["op"]&.downcase == "write" }.sum { |e| e["value"] || 0 }

        {
          cpu_percent: StatsCalculator.cpu_percent(raw),
          memory_mb: StatsCalculator.memory_mb(raw),
          memory_limit_mb: (raw.dig("memory_stats", "limit") || 0) / 1_048_576.0,
          net_rx: net_rx,
          net_tx: net_tx,
          disk_read: disk_read,
          disk_write: disk_write,
          pids: raw.dig("pids_stats", "current") || 0
        }
      end
    end

    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { render partial: "sandbox_stats", locals: { stats: @stats, sandbox: sandbox } }
      format.json { render json: @stats ? { cpu: @stats[:cpu_percent], mem: @stats[:memory_mb].round(1) } : { cpu: nil, mem: nil } }
    end
  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { render partial: "sandbox_stats", locals: { stats: nil, sandbox: nil } }
      format.json { render json: { cpu: nil, mem: nil } }
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    respond_to do |format|
      format.html { render partial: "sandbox_stats", locals: { stats: nil, sandbox: sandbox } }
      format.json { render json: { cpu: nil, mem: nil } }
    end
  end

  private

  def running_vnc_ids
    Dir.glob(File.join(VncManager::DYNAMIC_DIR, "vnc-*.yml")).filter_map { |f|
      File.basename(f).match(/\Avnc-(\d+)\.yml\z/)&.[](1)&.to_i
    }.to_set
  rescue
    Set.new
  end

  def tailscale_ips_for(sandboxes)
    ts_sandboxes = sandboxes.select { |s| s.tailscale? && s.container_id.present? }
    return {} if ts_sandboxes.empty?

    all_containers = Docker::Container.all
    container_map = all_containers.each_with_object({}) { |c, h| h[c.id] = c }

    ts_sandboxes.each_with_object({}) do |sandbox, ips|
      container = container_map[sandbox.container_id]
      next unless container
      network = sandbox.user.tailscale_network
      next unless network.present?
      ip = container.info.dig("NetworkSettings", "Networks", network, "IPAddress")
      ips[sandbox.id] = ip if ip.present?
    end
  rescue Docker::Error::DockerError
    {}
  end
end

class User < ApplicationRecord
  has_secure_password
  encrypts :smb_password

  def smb_password
    super
  rescue ActiveRecord::Encryption::Errors::Decryption
    nil
  end
  has_many :sessions, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :sandboxes, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :api_tokens, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :oauth_identities, dependent: :destroy

  normalizes :email_address, with: ->(e) { e.strip.downcase }
  normalizes :name, with: ->(n) { n.strip.downcase }

  validates :name, presence: true, uniqueness: true,
    format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{1,30}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric (2-31 chars, start with letter)" }
  validates :email_address, presence: true, uniqueness: true
  validates :status, inclusion: { in: %w[active suspended pending_approval] }
  validates :sandbox_archive_retention_days, numericality: { only_integer: true, greater_than_or_equal_to: 0, allow_nil: true }
  validates :terminal_emulator, inclusion: { in: %w[ghostty xterm], allow_nil: true }

  scope :active, -> { where(status: "active") }

  def admin?
    admin
  end

  def active?
    status == "active"
  end

  def suspended?
    status == "suspended"
  end

  def pending_approval?
    status == "pending_approval"
  end

  def tailscale_enabled?
    respond_to?(:tailscale_state) && tailscale_state == "enabled"
  end

  def tailscale_pending?
    respond_to?(:tailscale_state) && tailscale_state == "pending"
  end

  def tailscale_disabled?
    !respond_to?(:tailscale_state) || tailscale_state == "disabled"
  end

  def tailscale_auto_connect?
    tailscale_auto_connect
  end

  def chrome_persist_profile?
    chrome_persist_profile
  end

  def effective_archive_retention_days
    return sandbox_archive_retention_days if sandbox_archive_retention_days.present?

    @effective_archive_retention_days_fallback ||= Setting.instance.sandbox_archive_retention_days || 30
  end
end

def effective_archive_retention_days
    return sandbox_archive_retention_days if sandbox_archive_retention_days.present?

    @effective_archive_retention_days_fallback ||= Setting.instance.sandbox_archive_retention_days || 30
  end

  CUSTOM_LINK_SHOW_ON_VALUES = %w[all desktop tablet phone].freeze
  CUSTOM_LINK_TEMPLATE_VARS = %w[user hostname ssh_port sandbox tailscale_ip].freeze

  def expand_custom_link_url(url_template, sandbox:, tailscale_ip: nil)
    hostname = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    url_template.gsub(/\{(\w+)\}/) do |match|
      case $1
      when "user" then name
      when "hostname" then hostname
      when "ssh_port" then sandbox.ssh_port.to_s
      when "sandbox" then sandbox.name
      when "tailscale_ip" then tailscale_ip.to_s
      else match
      end
    end
  end

  private

  def validate_custom_links
    return if custom_links.blank?
    unless custom_links.is_a?(Array)
      errors.add(:custom_links, "must be an array")
      return
    end
    custom_links.each_with_index do |link, i|
      unless link.is_a?(Hash) && link["name"].present? && link["url"].present?
        errors.add(:custom_links, "entry #{i + 1} must have a name and url")
      end
      if link["show_on"].present? && !CUSTOM_LINK_SHOW_ON_VALUES.include?(link["show_on"])
        errors.add(:custom_links, "entry #{i + 1} show_on must be one of: #{CUSTOM_LINK_SHOW_ON_VALUES.join(', ')}")
      end
    end
  end
end

  def validate_custom_links
    return if custom_links.blank?
    unless custom_links.is_a?(Array)
      errors.add(:custom_links, "must be an array")
      return
    end
    custom_links.each_with_index do |link, i|
      unless link.is_a?(Hash) && link["name"].present? && link["url"].present?
        errors.add(:custom_links, "entry #{i + 1} must have a name and url")
      end
      if link["show_on"].present? && !CUSTOM_LINK_SHOW_ON_VALUES.include?(link["show_on"])
        errors.add(:custom_links, "entry #{i + 1} show_on must be one of: #{CUSTOM_LINK_SHOW_ON_VALUES.join(', ')}")
      end
    end
  end
end

class User < ApplicationRecord
  has_secure_password
  encrypts :smb_password

  def smb_password
    super
  rescue ActiveRecord::Encryption::Errors::Decryption
    nil
  end
  has_many :sessions, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :sandboxes, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :api_tokens, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :oauth_identities, dependent: :destroy

  normalizes :email_address, with: ->(e) { e.strip.downcase }
  normalizes :name, with: ->(n) { n.strip.downcase }

  validates :name, presence: true, uniqueness: true,
    format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{1,30}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric (2-31 chars, start with letter)" }
  validates :email_address, presence: true, uniqueness: true
  validates :status, inclusion: { in: %w[active suspended pending_approval] }
  validates :sandbox_archive_retention_days, numericality: { only_integer: true, greater_than_or_equal_to: 0, allow_nil: true }
  validates :terminal_emulator, inclusion: { in: %w[ghostty xterm], allow_nil: true }
  validate :validate_custom_links

  scope :active, -> { where(status: "active") }

  def admin?
    admin
  end

  def active?
    status == "active"
  end

  def suspended?
    status == "suspended"
  end

  def pending_approval?
    status == "pending_approval"
  end

  def tailscale_enabled?
    respond_to?(:tailscale_state) && tailscale_state == "enabled"
  end

  def tailscale_pending?
    respond_to?(:tailscale_state) && tailscale_state == "pending"
  end

  def tailscale_disabled?
    !respond_to?(:tailscale_state) || tailscale_state == "disabled"
  end

  def tailscale_auto_connect?
    tailscale_auto_connect
  end

  def chrome_persist_profile?
    chrome_persist_profile
  end

  def effective_archive_retention_days
    return sandbox_archive_retention_days if sandbox_archive_retention_days.present?

    @effective_archive_retention_days_fallback ||= Setting.instance.sandbox_archive_retention_days || 30
  end
end

Rails.application.routes.draw do
  root "dashboard#index"

  # Mount Action Cable for WebSocket connections
  mount ActionCable.server => "/cable"

  resource :session
  resources :passwords, param: :token

  get  "invites/:token",        to: "registrations#new",    as: :new_registration
  post "invites/:token/accept", to: "registrations#create", as: :accept_registration

  get  "auth/:provider/callback", to: "oauth_callbacks#create"
  post "auth/:provider/callback", to: "oauth_callbacks#create"
  get  "auth/failure",            to: "oauth_callbacks#failure"
  resource :change_password, only: [ :show, :update ]

  resource :settings, only: :show do
    patch :update_profile
    patch :update_password
    patch :toggle_tailscale
    patch :update_smb_password
    post :generate_token
    delete "revoke_token/:id", action: :revoke_token, as: :revoke_token
  end

  get  "auth/device",              to: "device_auth#show",     as: :auth_device
  post "auth/device/verify",       to: "device_auth#verify",   as: :auth_device_verify
  get  "auth/device/approve/:id",  to: "device_auth#confirm",  as: :auth_device_confirm
  post "auth/device/approve",      to: "device_auth#approve",  as: :auth_device_approve

  resources :sandboxes, only: [ :new, :create, :show, :destroy, :update ] do
    member do
      post :start
      post :stop
      post :rebuild
      post :retry
      post :archive_restore
      delete :purge
      get :logs
      get :stats, controller: "dashboard", action: "stats"
      get :metrics
      get :card, controller: "dashboard", action: "card"
      post :terminal, controller: "terminal", action: "open"
      delete :terminal, controller: "terminal", action: "close"
      post :vnc, controller: "vnc", action: "open"
      delete :vnc, controller: "vnc", action: "close"
      post :snapshot, controller: "snapshots", action: "create_for_sandbox"
    end
    resources :routes, only: [ :create, :destroy ]
  end

  resources :snapshots, only: [ :index, :destroy ], param: :name do
    member do
      post :clone
    end
  end

  get "terminals/:id/:type", to: "terminal#show", as: :terminal_show, constraints: { type: /tmux|shell/ }
  get "terminal/auth", to: "terminal#auth"
  get "vnc/auth", to: "vnc#auth"

  resource :tailscale, only: [], controller: "tailscale" do
    get :show
    post :login
    get :login_status
    get :connected
    patch :update_settings
    delete :disable
  end

  namespace :admin do
    get "/", to: "dashboard#index", as: :dashboard
    get "system_status", to: "dashboard#system_status"
    get "update_status", to: "dashboard#update_status"
    resource :update, only: [], controller: "update" do
      get  :check
      post :pull
      get  :status
      post :restart
      get  :progress
    end
    resource :settings, only: [ :edit, :update ]
    resources :users
    resources :invites, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ]
    resources :sandboxes, only: :destroy do
      member do
        post :start
        post :stop
        post :rebuild
        get :stats
        post :archive_restore
        delete :purge
      end
    end

    resources :docker, only: :index, controller: "docker" do
      get :logs, on: :member
    end

    # Job monitoring dashboard
    mount MissionControl::Jobs::Engine, at: "/jobs"

    # Error tracking dashboard
    mount SolidErrors::Engine, at: "/errors"
  end

  namespace :api do
    get "archived_sandboxes", to: "sandboxes#archived_index"
    resources :sandboxes do
      member do
        post :start
        post :stop
        post :rebuild
        get :logs
        post :connect
        post :snapshot
        post :restore
        post :archive_restore
        delete :purge
        post :tailscale_connect
        delete :tailscale_disconnect
        post "services/:service/start", action: :service_start, as: :service_start
        post "services/:service/stop", action: :service_stop, as: :service_stop
      end
      resources :routes, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ]
    end
    resources :snapshots, only: [ :index, :create, :show, :destroy ], param: :name
    resources :users
    resource :status, only: :show, controller: "status"
    resource :info, only: :show
    resources :tokens, only: [ :index, :create, :destroy ]
    namespace :auth do
      post :device_code
      post :device_token
    end
    resource :tailscale, only: [], controller: "tailscale" do
      post :enable
      post :login
      get :login_status
      patch :update_settings
      delete :disable
      get :status
    end
    resource :smb, only: [], controller: "smb" do
      patch :set_password
    end
  end

  get "guide", to: "pages#guide"

  get "up" => "rails/health#show", as: :rails_health_check
end

class SettingsController < ApplicationController
  def show
    @user = Current.user
    @api_tokens = @user.api_tokens.active.order(created_at: :desc)
  end

  def update_profile
    @user = Current.user

    if @user.update(profile_params)
      redirect_to settings_path, notice: "Profile updated successfully."
    else
      render :show, status: :unprocessable_entity
    end
  end

  def update_password
    @user = Current.user

    unless @user.authenticate(params[:current_password])
      redirect_to settings_path, alert: "Current password is incorrect."
      return
    end

    if @user.update(password_params)
      redirect_to settings_path, notice: "Password changed successfully."
    else
      redirect_to settings_path, alert: @user.errors.full_messages.join(", ")
    end
  end

  def update_smb_password
    @user = Current.user

    if @user.update(smb_password_params)
      SandboxManager.new.update_smb_password(user: @user)
      redirect_to settings_path, notice: "SMB password updated."
    else
      redirect_to settings_path, alert: @user.errors.full_messages.join(", ").presence || "Failed to update SMB password."
    end
  end

  def toggle_tailscale
    @user = Current.user

    if @user.update(tailscale_auto_connect: !@user.tailscale_auto_connect)
      status = @user.tailscale_auto_connect ? "enabled" : "disabled"
      redirect_to settings_path, notice: "Tailscale auto-connect #{status}."
    else
      redirect_to settings_path, alert: "Failed to update Tailscale settings."
    end
  end

  def generate_token
    @user = Current.user

    token_name = params[:name].presence || "Web UI Token"
    token, raw_token = ApiToken.generate_for(@user, name: token_name)

    flash[:api_token] = raw_token
    redirect_to settings_path, notice: "API token '#{token_name}' generated. Make sure to copy it now - you won't be able to see it again!"
  end

  def revoke_token
    @user = Current.user
    token = @user.api_tokens.find(params[:id])
    token_name = token.name
    token.destroy!

    redirect_to settings_path, notice: "API token '#{token_name}' revoked."
  end

  private

  def profile_params
    params.require(:user).permit(:email_address, :full_name, :github_username, :chrome_persist_profile, :sandbox_archive_retention_days, :terminal_emulator)
  end

  def password_params
    params.require(:user).permit(:password, :password_confirmation)
  end

  def smb_password_params
    params.require(:user).permit(:smb_password)
  end
end

1 <% content_for(:title) { "Getting Started — Sandcastle" } %> 2 3
4

Getting Started

5

Everything you need to go from zero to a running sandbox.

6 7 <% host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "sandcastle.example.com") %> 8 9 <%# ── Install ── %> 10
11

1. Install the CLI

12

13 Download the latest release from 14 <%= link_to "GitHub Releases", "https://github.com/thieso2/Sandcastle/releases/latest", target: "_blank", class: "text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800 underline" %>, 15 then extract it: 16

17 
tar xzf sandcastle_*.tar.gz
18	sudo mv sandcastle /usr/local/bin/
19
20 21 <%# ── Login ── %> 22
23

2. Log in

24

Point the CLI at this server and authenticate via your browser:

25 
sandcastle login https://<%= host %>
26
27 28 <%# ── Tailscale ── %> 29
30

3. Enable Tailscale (recommended)

31

32 Each sandbox gets its own Tailscale IP so you can reach services directly — no port forwarding needed. 33

34 
# Interactive login — opens a browser URL to authenticate
35	sandcastle tailscale enable
36	
37	# Or use an auth key for headless setups
38	sandcastle tailscale enable --auth-key tskey-auth-...
39

After authenticating, approve the advertised subnet routes in the Tailscale admin console.

40
41 42 <%# ── Create ── %> 43
44

4. Create a sandbox

45 46

Web UI — click the "Create Sandcastle" button on your dashboard to create a sandbox with all available options:

47
    48
  • Name — unique identifier for your sandbox
    • 49
  • Container Image — custom image or default
    • 50
  • Snapshot — restore from an existing snapshot
    • 51
  • Mount Home — persistent home directory across all sandboxes
    • 52
  • Data Path — mount user data (or subpath) to /persisted
    • 53
  • Tailscale — connect to your Tailscale network
    • 54
  • Docker daemon — enable/disable Docker-in-Docker
    • 55
  • Temporary — auto-remove when you disconnect
    • 56
57 58

Or use the CLI for quick creation:

59 60

Quick throwaway sandbox — deleted when you disconnect:

61 
sandcastle create scratch --rm
62 63

Persistent sandbox — keeps your home directory and data across restarts:

64 
sandcastle create my-dev --home --data
65 66

Lightweight sandbox — no Docker daemon or VNC (faster startup):

67 
sandcastle create lean --no-docker --no-vnc
68 69
70 Tip: Make these flags the default with config or env vars:
71 sandcastle config set mount_home true
72 sandcastle config set data_path .
73 sandcastle config set vnc false
74 sandcastle config set docker false
75 Or via env: export SANDCASTLE_HOME=1 SANDCASTLE_DATA=. SANDCASTLE_VNC=false 76
77
78 79 <%# ── Connect ── %> 80
81

5. Connect

82

Two ways to get a shell:

83 
# tmux session (reconnectable, auto-starts stopped sandboxes)
84	sandcastle connect my-dev
85	
86	# Plain SSH (no tmux)
87	sandcastle ssh my-dev
88	
89	# Pass extra SSH options after --
90	sandcastle connect my-dev -- -L 8080:localhost:8080
91	sandcastle ssh my-dev -- -D 1080
92 93

SSH agent forwarding

94

95 The CLI automatically forwards your local SSH agent (-A) 96 so you can use your SSH keys (e.g. for Git) inside the sandbox without copying them. 97 This works with the default SSH protocol. 98

99 100

Mosh (persistent, roaming sessions)

101

102 Use mosh 103 for a connection that survives network changes and high-latency links. Mosh is pre-installed in every sandbox. 104

105 
sandcastle connect --mosh my-dev
106
107 Warning: Mosh does not support SSH agent forwarding. 108 Mosh drops the SSH connection after bootstrap and switches to UDP, which has no agent forwarding support. 109 Your ssh-add -l keys will not be available inside the sandbox. 110 If you need agent forwarding (e.g. for Git over SSH), use the default SSH protocol instead. 111
112
113 Requirements: mosh must be installed on your local machine 114 (brew install mosh on macOS, 115 apt install mosh on Debian/Ubuntu). 116 Mosh uses UDP ports 60000–61000 — if your sandbox is reachable only via SSH tunneling, use 117 Tailscale instead (it handles UDP natively). 118
119
120 121 <%# ── Web Terminal ── %> 122
123

6. Web terminal

124

125 Click tmux or shell on any running sandbox to open a browser-based terminal. 126 No SSH client needed — just a web browser. 127

128
129 How it works: ttyd runs inside each sandbox container, exposing two terminal endpoints: 130 tmux (persistent, re-attachable session shared across browser tabs) and 131 shell (a plain login shell). 132 Both are protected by your existing login — no extra passwords required. 133
134
135 136 <%# ── VNC ── %> 137
138

7. VNC

139

140 Click the VNC button on any running sandbox to access Google Chrome with a graphical desktop environment. 141 Perfect for browser automation, visual testing, or running GUI applications. 142

143
144 How it works: Each sandbox includes Google Chrome, Xvfb (virtual X server), and x11vnc. 145 Clicking VNC launches a noVNC container that connects to the sandbox's VNC server, giving you a full graphical browser in your web browser. 146
147
148 Chrome profile persistence: Go to Settings → VNC to enable Chrome profile persistence. 149 When enabled, your Chrome extensions, bookmarks, and settings are saved and persist across sandbox recreations. 150 If you use Mount Home, profiles are stored in ~/.config/google-chrome alongside all your other home directory files. 151 Without Mount Home, profiles are stored separately in a dedicated directory. 152
153
154 155 <%# ── SMB File Sharing ── %> 156
157

8. SMB file sharing

158

159 Mount your sandbox home directory or /persisted 160 as a network drive from macOS, Windows, or Linux using SMB3. 161 This is useful for editing files with your local IDE or dragging and dropping in Finder/Explorer. 162

163
164 Requirements: Tailscale must be enabled (for network access) and you must set an SMB password in 165 <%= link_to "Settings → File Sharing", settings_path, class: "underline" %>. 166
167 168

Setup

169 
# Set your SMB password (stored encrypted on the server)
170	sandcastle smb set-password
171	
172	# Create a sandbox with SMB enabled
173	sandcastle create my-dev --smb --tailscale
174 175

Connect

176 
# macOS (Finder → Go → Connect to Server, or in Terminal):
177	open smb://<tailscale-ip>/home
178	open smb://<tailscale-ip>/persisted   # if --data was used
179	
180	# Windows (File Explorer → Map Network Drive):
181	\\<tailscale-ip>\home
182	
183	# Linux:
184	sudo mount -t cifs //<tailscale-ip>/home /mnt/sandbox \
185	  -o username=<your-sandcastle-username>,vers=3.0
186

187 The Tailscale IP is shown on the sandbox detail page. Username is your Sandcastle username. Password is what you set with sandcastle smb set-password. 188

189
190 191 <%# ── Manage ── %> 192
193

9. Manage sandboxes

194 
sandcastle list              # Show all your sandboxes
195	sandcastle stop my-dev       # Stop a running sandbox
196	sandcastle start my-dev      # Start a stopped sandbox
197	sandcastle rebuild my-dev    # Recreate with latest image
198	sandcastle rename my-dev new # Rename a sandbox
199	sandcastle delete my-dev     # Archive sandbox (soft-delete, data kept)
200	
201	# Copy files to/from a sandbox
202	sandcastle cp file.txt my-dev:~/           # local → sandbox
203	sandcastle cp my-dev:~/data.csv .          # sandbox → local
204	sandcastle cp -r my-dev:~/project ./       # recursive
205 206

Archive & restore

207

208 When you delete a sandbox it is archived rather than immediately purged. 209 Archived sandboxes have no running container but their data is preserved on disk. 210 After the retention period (default: 30 days) a background job permanently deletes them. 211 Admins can adjust the system-wide retention on the Settings page, and each user can override it 212 in their profile settings. 213

214 
# List archived sandboxes
215	sandcastle list --archived
216	
217	# Restore an archived sandbox (recreates and starts the container)
218	sandcastle unarchive my-dev
219	
220	# Permanently delete an archived sandbox immediately, bypassing the retention period
221	sandcastle purge my-dev
222

223 Archived sandboxes also appear as a collapsible section on your dashboard with a Restore button. 224 The sandbox name is freed immediately on archive, so you can create a new sandbox with the same name. 225

226
227 228 <%# ── Routes ── %> 229
230

10. Routes

231

232 Expose services running inside your sandbox to the outside world. Two modes are supported: 233

234 235

HTTP routes (layer 7, custom domain + TLS)

236

237 Route a custom domain to a port inside the sandbox. Traefik terminates TLS automatically. 238

239 
# Add an HTTP route — app.example.com → container port 8080 (default)
240	sandcastle route add myapp app.example.com
241	
242	# Add an HTTP route on a custom container port
243	sandcastle route add myapp api.example.com 3000
244

Point a DNS A/CNAME record at <%= host %> before adding the route. TLS certificates are provisioned automatically.

245 246

TCP routes (layer 4, raw port forward)

247

248 Forward a public TCP port (3000–3099) directly to a container port. Useful for databases, 249 message brokers, or any non-HTTP service. 250

251 
# Forward a public port to container port 5432 (e.g. Postgres)
252	sandcastle route add myapp --tcp 5432
253	# Output: TCP port 3042 → container port 5432
254

A public port from the pool is assigned automatically. Connect with <%= host %>:<public-port>.

255 256

Managing routes

257 
# List all routes for a sandbox (shows ID, mode, domain/port, container port)
258	sandcastle route list myapp
259	
260	# Remove an HTTP route by domain
261	sandcastle route delete myapp app.example.com
262	
263	# Remove any route by ID (use the ID from route list)
264	sandcastle route delete myapp --id 42
265

Routes can also be managed from the sandbox detail page in the web UI.

266
267 268 <%# ── Snapshots ── %> 269
270

11. Snapshots

271

272 Save and restore the full state of a sandbox. Snapshots are composite — they capture 273 the container filesystem (via docker commit) 274 and, when on a BTRFS filesystem, also the home directory and data directory. 275

276 
# Create a snapshot (all available layers)
277	sandcastle snapshot create my-dev before-migration
278	
279	# Create with a description and specific layers
280	sandcastle snapshot create my-dev checkpoint --label "before big refactor" --layers container,home
281	
282	# List all snapshots
283	sandcastle snapshot list
284	
285	# Show details of a specific snapshot
286	sandcastle snapshot show before-migration
287	
288	# Restore a snapshot into an existing sandbox (all layers)
289	sandcastle snapshot restore my-dev before-migration
290	
291	# Restore only the container layer
292	sandcastle snapshot restore my-dev before-migration --layers container
293	
294	# Create a new sandbox from a snapshot (all layers)
295	sandcastle create my-dev-v2 --from-snapshot before-migration
296	
297	# Clone only the container layer into a new sandbox
298	sandcastle create my-dev-v2 --from-snapshot before-migration --restore-layers container
299	
300	# Delete a snapshot
301	sandcastle snapshot delete before-migration
302

303 Web UI: Each sandbox has a detail page (click the sandbox name) with a Snapshots tab 304 for creating, viewing, and deleting snapshots. The global 305 <%= link_to "Snapshots page", snapshots_path, class: "text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800 underline" %> 306 shows all snapshots across all your sandboxes with clone-to-new-sandbox support. 307

308 <% unless (BtrfsHelper.btrfs? rescue false) %> 309
310 Note: BTRFS not detected on this host. Only the container layer is snapshotted. 311 Home and data layers require BTRFS. 312
313 <% end %> 314
315 316 <%# ── Settings ── %> 317
318

12. User settings

319

320 Click Settings in the navbar to manage your account preferences: 321

322
    323
  • Profile — Update your email address and change your password
    • 324
  • Tailscale — View connection status and toggle auto-connect for new sandboxes
    • 325
  • Sandbox Archive — Set your personal archive retention period (overrides the system default)
    • 326
  • VNC — Toggle Chrome profile persistence across sandbox recreations
    • 327
  • API Tokens — Create, list, and revoke multiple named API tokens for different devices or applications
    • 328
329
330 Using API tokens: Create tokens for different use cases (laptop, CI/CD, mobile).
331 Via Web UI: Go to Settings → click "New Token" → give it a name → copy the token
332 Via CLI: Run sandcastle token create laptop to create a named token
333 sandcastle token list to view all your tokens
334 sandcastle token revoke <id> to revoke a specific token 335
336
337 338 <%# ── Inviting Users (Admin) ── %> 339
340

13. Inviting users (admin)

341

342 Admins can invite new users via email. Each invite generates a unique, one-time registration link. 343 New users click the link to choose a username and set a password — no pre-created account required. 344

345

To send an invite:

346
    347
  1. Go to Admin → Invites
    2. 348
  2. Click Invite User
    3. 349
  3. Enter the invitee's email address and an optional personal message
    4. 350
  4. Click Send Invite — the invitee will receive an email with a registration link
    5. 351
352

Invite lifecycle:

353
    354
  • Pending — sent but not yet accepted; link is still valid
    • 355
  • Accepted — user has registered; link is invalidated
    • 356
  • Expired — link expired (default: 7 days) before being accepted
    • 357
358

359 Pending invites can be revoked from the Invites admin panel at any time. 360 Once revoked, the registration link immediately becomes invalid. 361

362
363 364 <%# ── Custom SSL Certificates ── %> 365
366

14. Custom SSL certificates (advanced)

367

368 For internal networks, you can configure alternative hostnames with custom SSL certificates to avoid browser warnings. 369

370 371

Generate certificates with mkcert:

372 
# Install mkcert (macOS example)
373	brew install mkcert
374	mkcert -install
375	
376	# Generate certificate for your domains
377	mkcert sandcastle.internal sc.local
378 379

Configure Sandcastle:

380

Add these variables to your sandcastle.env before installation:

381 
SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES=sandcastle.internal,sc.local
382	REDACTED.internal+1.pem
383	REDACTED.internal+1-key.pem
384	SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE=selfsigned
385 386

The installer will copy the certificates to $SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/traefik/certs/ and configure Traefik to serve them for the specified hostnames.

387 388
389 Note: You'll need to add DNS entries (or edit /etc/hosts) to point your alternative hostnames to the Sandcastle server IP. 390
391
392 393 <%# ── CLI Preferences ── %> 394
395

15. CLI preferences

396

397 Persist connection preferences in ~/.sandcastle/config.yaml. 398 Environment variables override the file (useful with 399 mise or direnv). 400

401 
# Use mosh instead of SSH by default
402	sandcastle config set connect_protocol mosh
403	
404	# Disable automatic tmux session attachment
405	sandcastle config set use_tmux false
406	
407	# Always mount persistent home directory on create
408	sandcastle config set mount_home true
409	
410	# Always mount user data directory on create
411	sandcastle config set data_path .
412	
413	# Disable VNC by default
414	sandcastle config set vnc false
415	
416	# Disable Docker (DinD) by default
417	sandcastle config set docker false
418	
419	# Show effective settings (merged from config file + env vars)
420	sandcastle config show
421 422

Environment variable overrides (highest priority):

423 
SANDCASTLE_CONNECT_PROTOCOL=mosh  # "ssh" (default) | "mosh"
424	SANDCASTLE_USE_TMUX=false         # "true" (default) | "false"
425	SANDCASTLE_SSH_EXTRA_ARGS="-v"    # extra flags appended to ssh/mosh
426	SANDCASTLE_HOME=1                 # mount persistent home on create
427	SANDCASTLE_DATA=.                 # mount user data dir on create
428	SANDCASTLE_VNC=false              # disable VNC on create
429	SANDCASTLE_DOCKER=false           # disable Docker (DinD) on create
430 431

Example: per-project override with mise:

432 
# .mise.toml
433	[env]
434	SANDCASTLE_CONNECT_PROTOCOL = "mosh"
435	SANDCASTLE_USE_TMUX = "false"
436
437 438
439

16. Short aliases

440

441 Common commands have 1-2 letter shortcuts: 442

443 
sandcastle cr        # create (temp sandbox)
444	sandcastle cr dev    # create dev
445	sandcastle c dev     # connect dev
446	sandcastle s dev     # ssh dev
447	sandcastle x dev -- cmd  # exec
448	sandcastle ls        # list
449	sandcastle rm dev    # delete dev
450	sandcastle mv dev new  # rename dev → new
451	sandcastle up dev    # start dev
452	sandcastle dn dev    # stop dev
453	sandcastle cp f dev:~/ # scp file to sandbox
454	sandcastle i         # info
455	sandcastle st        # status
456	sandcastle snap      # snapshot
457	sandcastle ts        # tailscale
458
459 460
461

462 Run sandcastle --help for the full command reference. 463

464
465
466

<% content_for(:title) { "Getting Started — Sandcastle" } %>

Getting Started

Everything you need to go from zero to a running sandbox.

<% host = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "sandcastle.example.com") %> <%# ── Install ── %>

1. Install the CLI

Download the latest release from <%= link_to "GitHub Releases", "https://github.com/thieso2/Sandcastle/releases/latest", target: "_blank", class: "text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800 underline" %>, then extract it: 

tar xzf sandcastle_*.tar.gz
sudo mv sandcastle /usr/local/bin/
<%# ── Login ── %>

2. Log in

Point the CLI at this server and authenticate via your browser:

sandcastle login https://<%= host %>
<%# ── Tailscale ── %>

3. Enable Tailscale (recommended)

Each sandbox gets its own Tailscale IP so you can reach services directly — no port forwarding needed. 

# Interactive login — opens a browser URL to authenticate
sandcastle tailscale enable

# Or use an auth key for headless setups
sandcastle tailscale enable --auth-key tskey-auth-...

After authenticating, approve the advertised subnet routes in the Tailscale admin console.

<%# ── Create ── %>

4. Create a sandbox

Web UI — click the "Create Sandcastle" button on your dashboard to create a sandbox with all available options:

  • Name — unique identifier for your sandbox
  • Container Image — custom image or default
  • Snapshot — restore from an existing snapshot
  • Mount Home — persistent home directory across all sandboxes
  • Data Path — mount user data (or subpath) to /persisted
  • Tailscale — connect to your Tailscale network
  • Docker daemon — enable/disable Docker-in-Docker
  • Temporary — auto-remove when you disconnect

Or use the CLI for quick creation:

Quick throwaway sandbox — deleted when you disconnect:

sandcastle create scratch --rm

Persistent sandbox — keeps your home directory and data across restarts:

sandcastle create my-dev --home --data

Lightweight sandbox — no Docker daemon or VNC (faster startup):

sandcastle create lean --no-docker --no-vnc
Tip: Make these flags the default with config or env vars:
sandcastle config set mount_home true
sandcastle config set data_path .
sandcastle config set vnc false
sandcastle config set docker false
Or via env: export SANDCASTLE_HOME=1 SANDCASTLE_DATA=. SANDCASTLE_VNC=false
<%# ── Connect ── %>

5. Connect

Two ways to get a shell:

# tmux session (reconnectable, auto-starts stopped sandboxes)
sandcastle connect my-dev

# Plain SSH (no tmux)
sandcastle ssh my-dev

# Pass extra SSH options after --
sandcastle connect my-dev -- -L 8080:localhost:8080
sandcastle ssh my-dev -- -D 1080

SSH agent forwarding

The CLI automatically forwards your local SSH agent (-A) so you can use your SSH keys (e.g. for Git) inside the sandbox without copying them. This works with the default SSH protocol.

Mosh (persistent, roaming sessions)

Use mosh for a connection that survives network changes and high-latency links. Mosh is pre-installed in every sandbox. 

sandcastle connect --mosh my-dev
Warning: Mosh does not support SSH agent forwarding. Mosh drops the SSH connection after bootstrap and switches to UDP, which has no agent forwarding support. Your ssh-add -l keys will not be available inside the sandbox. If you need agent forwarding (e.g. for Git over SSH), use the default SSH protocol instead.
Requirements: mosh must be installed on your local machine (brew install mosh on macOS, apt install mosh on Debian/Ubuntu). Mosh uses UDP ports 60000–61000 — if your sandbox is reachable only via SSH tunneling, use Tailscale instead (it handles UDP natively).
<%# ── Web Terminal ── %>

6. Web terminal

Click tmux or shell on any running sandbox to open a browser-based terminal. No SSH client needed — just a web browser.

How it works: ttyd runs inside each sandbox container, exposing two terminal endpoints: tmux (persistent, re-attachable session shared across browser tabs) and shell (a plain login shell). Both are protected by your existing login — no extra passwords required.
<%# ── VNC ── %>

7. VNC

Click the VNC button on any running sandbox to access Google Chrome with a graphical desktop environment. Perfect for browser automation, visual testing, or running GUI applications.

How it works: Each sandbox includes Google Chrome, Xvfb (virtual X server), and x11vnc. Clicking VNC launches a noVNC container that connects to the sandbox's VNC server, giving you a full graphical browser in your web browser.
Chrome profile persistence: Go to Settings → VNC to enable Chrome profile persistence. When enabled, your Chrome extensions, bookmarks, and settings are saved and persist across sandbox recreations. If you use Mount Home, profiles are stored in ~/.config/google-chrome alongside all your other home directory files. Without Mount Home, profiles are stored separately in a dedicated directory.
<%# ── SMB File Sharing ── %>

8. SMB file sharing

Mount your sandbox home directory or /persisted as a network drive from macOS, Windows, or Linux using SMB3. This is useful for editing files with your local IDE or dragging and dropping in Finder/Explorer.

Requirements: Tailscale must be enabled (for network access) and you must set an SMB password in <%= link_to "Settings → File Sharing", settings_path, class: "underline" %>.

Setup

# Set your SMB password (stored encrypted on the server)
sandcastle smb set-password

# Create a sandbox with SMB enabled
sandcastle create my-dev --smb --tailscale

Connect

# macOS (Finder → Go → Connect to Server, or in Terminal):
open smb://<tailscale-ip>/home
open smb://<tailscale-ip>/persisted   # if --data was used

# Windows (File Explorer → Map Network Drive):
\\<tailscale-ip>\home

# Linux:
sudo mount -t cifs //<tailscale-ip>/home /mnt/sandbox \
  -o username=<your-sandcastle-username>,vers=3.0

The Tailscale IP is shown on the sandbox detail page. Username is your Sandcastle username. Password is what you set with sandcastle smb set-password.

<%# ── Manage ── %>

9. Manage sandboxes

sandcastle list              # Show all your sandboxes
sandcastle stop my-dev       # Stop a running sandbox
sandcastle start my-dev      # Start a stopped sandbox
sandcastle rebuild my-dev    # Recreate with latest image
sandcastle rename my-dev new # Rename a sandbox
sandcastle delete my-dev     # Archive sandbox (soft-delete, data kept)

# Copy files to/from a sandbox
sandcastle cp file.txt my-dev:~/           # local → sandbox
sandcastle cp my-dev:~/data.csv .          # sandbox → local
sandcastle cp -r my-dev:~/project ./       # recursive

Archive & restore

When you delete a sandbox it is archived rather than immediately purged. Archived sandboxes have no running container but their data is preserved on disk. After the retention period (default: 30 days) a background job permanently deletes them. Admins can adjust the system-wide retention on the Settings page, and each user can override it in their profile settings. 

# List archived sandboxes
sandcastle list --archived

# Restore an archived sandbox (recreates and starts the container)
sandcastle unarchive my-dev

# Permanently delete an archived sandbox immediately, bypassing the retention period
sandcastle purge my-dev

Archived sandboxes also appear as a collapsible section on your dashboard with a Restore button. The sandbox name is freed immediately on archive, so you can create a new sandbox with the same name.

<%# ── Routes ── %>

10. Routes

Expose services running inside your sandbox to the outside world. Two modes are supported:

HTTP routes (layer 7, custom domain + TLS)

Route a custom domain to a port inside the sandbox. Traefik terminates TLS automatically. 

# Add an HTTP route — app.example.com → container port 8080 (default)
sandcastle route add myapp app.example.com

# Add an HTTP route on a custom container port
sandcastle route add myapp api.example.com 3000

Point a DNS A/CNAME record at <%= host %> before adding the route. TLS certificates are provisioned automatically.

TCP routes (layer 4, raw port forward)

Forward a public TCP port (3000–3099) directly to a container port. Useful for databases, message brokers, or any non-HTTP service. 

# Forward a public port to container port 5432 (e.g. Postgres)
sandcastle route add myapp --tcp 5432
# Output: TCP port 3042 → container port 5432

A public port from the pool is assigned automatically. Connect with <%= host %>:<public-port>.

Managing routes

# List all routes for a sandbox (shows ID, mode, domain/port, container port)
sandcastle route list myapp

# Remove an HTTP route by domain
sandcastle route delete myapp app.example.com

# Remove any route by ID (use the ID from route list)
sandcastle route delete myapp --id 42

Routes can also be managed from the sandbox detail page in the web UI.

<%# ── Snapshots ── %>

11. Snapshots

Save and restore the full state of a sandbox. Snapshots are composite — they capture the container filesystem (via docker commit) and, when on a BTRFS filesystem, also the home directory and data directory. 

# Create a snapshot (all available layers)
sandcastle snapshot create my-dev before-migration

# Create with a description and specific layers
sandcastle snapshot create my-dev checkpoint --label "before big refactor" --layers container,home

# List all snapshots
sandcastle snapshot list

# Show details of a specific snapshot
sandcastle snapshot show before-migration

# Restore a snapshot into an existing sandbox (all layers)
sandcastle snapshot restore my-dev before-migration

# Restore only the container layer
sandcastle snapshot restore my-dev before-migration --layers container

# Create a new sandbox from a snapshot (all layers)
sandcastle create my-dev-v2 --from-snapshot before-migration

# Clone only the container layer into a new sandbox
sandcastle create my-dev-v2 --from-snapshot before-migration --restore-layers container

# Delete a snapshot
sandcastle snapshot delete before-migration

Web UI: Each sandbox has a detail page (click the sandbox name) with a Snapshots tab for creating, viewing, and deleting snapshots. The global <%= link_to "Snapshots page", snapshots_path, class: "text-blue-600 hover:text-blue-800 underline" %> shows all snapshots across all your sandboxes with clone-to-new-sandbox support.

<% unless (BtrfsHelper.btrfs? rescue false) %>
Note: BTRFS not detected on this host. Only the container layer is snapshotted. Home and data layers require BTRFS.
<% end %>
<%# ── Settings ── %>

12. User settings

Click Settings in the navbar to manage your account preferences:

  • Profile — Update your email address and change your password
  • Tailscale — View connection status and toggle auto-connect for new sandboxes
  • Sandbox Archive — Set your personal archive retention period (overrides the system default)
  • VNC — Toggle Chrome profile persistence across sandbox recreations
  • API Tokens — Create, list, and revoke multiple named API tokens for different devices or applications
Using API tokens: Create tokens for different use cases (laptop, CI/CD, mobile).
Via Web UI: Go to Settings → click "New Token" → give it a name → copy the token
Via CLI: Run sandcastle token create laptop to create a named token
sandcastle token list to view all your tokens
sandcastle token revoke <id> to revoke a specific token
<%# ── Inviting Users (Admin) ── %>

13. Inviting users (admin)

Admins can invite new users via email. Each invite generates a unique, one-time registration link. New users click the link to choose a username and set a password — no pre-created account required.

To send an invite:

  1. Go to Admin → Invites
  2. Click Invite User
  3. Enter the invitee's email address and an optional personal message
  4. Click Send Invite — the invitee will receive an email with a registration link

Invite lifecycle:

  • Pending — sent but not yet accepted; link is still valid
  • Accepted — user has registered; link is invalidated
  • Expired — link expired (default: 7 days) before being accepted

Pending invites can be revoked from the Invites admin panel at any time. Once revoked, the registration link immediately becomes invalid.

<%# ── Custom SSL Certificates ── %>

14. Custom SSL certificates (advanced)

For internal networks, you can configure alternative hostnames with custom SSL certificates to avoid browser warnings.

Generate certificates with mkcert:

# Install mkcert (macOS example)
brew install mkcert
mkcert -install

# Generate certificate for your domains
mkcert sandcastle.internal sc.local

Configure Sandcastle:

Add these variables to your sandcastle.env before installation:

SANDCASTLE_ALT_HOSTNAMES=sandcastle.internal,sc.local
REDACTED.internal+1.pem
REDACTED.internal+1-key.pem
SANDCASTLE_TLS_MODE=selfsigned

The installer will copy the certificates to $SANDCASTLE_HOME/data/traefik/certs/ and configure Traefik to serve them for the specified hostnames.

Note: You'll need to add DNS entries (or edit /etc/hosts) to point your alternative hostnames to the Sandcastle server IP.
<%# ── CLI Preferences ── %>

15. CLI preferences

Persist connection preferences in ~/.sandcastle/config.yaml. Environment variables override the file (useful with mise or direnv). 

# Use mosh instead of SSH by default
sandcastle config set connect_protocol mosh

# Disable automatic tmux session attachment
sandcastle config set use_tmux false

# Always mount persistent home directory on create
sandcastle config set mount_home true

# Always mount user data directory on create
sandcastle config set data_path .

# Disable VNC by default
sandcastle config set vnc false

# Disable Docker (DinD) by default
sandcastle config set docker false

# Show effective settings (merged from config file + env vars)
sandcastle config show

Environment variable overrides (highest priority):

SANDCASTLE_CONNECT_PROTOCOL=mosh  # "ssh" (default) | "mosh"
SANDCASTLE_USE_TMUX=false         # "true" (default) | "false"
SANDCASTLE_SSH_EXTRA_ARGS="-v"    # extra flags appended to ssh/mosh
SANDCASTLE_HOME=1                 # mount persistent home on create
SANDCASTLE_DATA=.                 # mount user data dir on create
SANDCASTLE_VNC=false              # disable VNC on create
SANDCASTLE_DOCKER=false           # disable Docker (DinD) on create

Example: per-project override with mise:

# .mise.toml
[env]
SANDCASTLE_CONNECT_PROTOCOL = "mosh"
SANDCASTLE_USE_TMUX = "false"

16. Short aliases

Common commands have 1-2 letter shortcuts: 

sandcastle cr        # create (temp sandbox)
sandcastle cr dev    # create dev
sandcastle c dev     # connect dev
sandcastle s dev     # ssh dev
sandcastle x dev -- cmd  # exec
sandcastle ls        # list
sandcastle rm dev    # delete dev
sandcastle mv dev new  # rename dev → new
sandcastle up dev    # start dev
sandcastle dn dev    # stop dev
sandcastle cp f dev:~/ # scp file to sandbox
sandcastle i         # info
sandcastle st        # status
sandcastle snap      # snapshot
sandcastle ts        # tailscale

Run sandcastle --help for the full command reference.

class AddSshKeysToUsers < ActiveRecord::Migration[8.1]
  def up
    add_column :users, :ssh_keys, :jsonb, default: []

    # Migrate existing ssh_public_key data into the new ssh_keys array
    execute <<~SQL
      UPDATE users
      SET ssh_keys = jsonb_build_array(jsonb_build_object('name', 'default', 'key', ssh_public_key))
      WHERE ssh_public_key IS NOT NULL AND ssh_public_key != ''
    SQL
  end

  def down
    remove_column :users, :ssh_keys
  end
end

class User < ApplicationRecord
  has_secure_password
  encrypts :smb_password

  def smb_password
    super
  rescue ActiveRecord::Encryption::Errors::Decryption
    nil
  end
  has_many :sessions, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :sandboxes, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :api_tokens, dependent: :destroy
  has_many :oauth_identities, dependent: :destroy

  normalizes :email_address, with: ->(e) { e.strip.downcase }
  normalizes :name, with: ->(n) { n.strip.downcase }

  validates :name, presence: true, uniqueness: true,
    format: { with: /\A[a-z][a-z0-9_-]{1,30}\z/, message: "must be lowercase alphanumeric (2-31 chars, start with letter)" }
  validates :email_address, presence: true, uniqueness: true
  validates :status, inclusion: { in: %w[active suspended pending_approval] }
  validates :sandbox_archive_retention_days, numericality: { only_integer: true, greater_than_or_equal_to: 0, allow_nil: true }
  validates :terminal_emulator, inclusion: { in: %w[ghostty xterm], allow_nil: true }
  validate :validate_custom_links

  scope :active, -> { where(status: "active") }

  def admin?
    admin
  end

  def active?
    status == "active"
  end

  def suspended?
    status == "suspended"
  end

  def pending_approval?
    status == "pending_approval"
  end

  def tailscale_enabled?
    respond_to?(:tailscale_state) && tailscale_state == "enabled"
  end

  def tailscale_pending?
    respond_to?(:tailscale_state) && tailscale_state == "pending"
  end

  def tailscale_disabled?
    !respond_to?(:tailscale_state) || tailscale_state == "disabled"
  end

  def tailscale_auto_connect?
    tailscale_auto_connect
  end

  def chrome_persist_profile?
    chrome_persist_profile
  end

  def effective_archive_retention_days
    return sandbox_archive_retention_days if sandbox_archive_retention_days.present?

    @effective_archive_retention_days_fallback ||= Setting.instance.sandbox_archive_retention_days || 30
  end

  CUSTOM_LINK_SHOW_ON_VALUES = %w[all desktop tablet phone].freeze
  CUSTOM_LINK_TEMPLATE_VARS = %w[user hostname ssh_port sandbox tailscale_ip].freeze

  def expand_custom_link_url(url_template, sandbox:, tailscale_ip: nil)
    hostname = ENV.fetch("SANDCASTLE_HOST", "localhost")
    url_template.gsub(/\{(\w+)\}/) do |match|
      case $1
      when "user" then name
      when "hostname" then hostname
      when "ssh_port" then sandbox.ssh_port.to_s
      when "sandbox" then sandbox.name
      when "tailscale_ip" then tailscale_ip.to_s
      else match
      end
    end
  end

  private

  def validate_custom_links
    return if custom_links.blank?
    unless custom_links.is_a?(Array)
      errors.add(:custom_links, "must be an array")
      return
    end
    custom_links.each_with_index do |link, i|
      unless link.is_a?(Hash) && link["name"].present? && link["url"].present?
        errors.add(:custom_links, "entry #{i + 1} must have a name and url")
      end
      if link["show_on"].present? && !CUSTOM_LINK_SHOW_ON_VALUES.include?(link["show_on"])
        errors.add(:custom_links, "entry #{i + 1} show_on must be one of: #{CUSTOM_LINK_SHOW_ON_VALUES.join(', ')}")
      end
    end
  end
end

input.old_string: def validate_custom_links
input.new_string:
def validate_ssh_keys
    return if ssh_keys.blank?
    unless ssh_keys.is_a?(Array)
      errors.add(:ssh_keys, "must be an array")
      return
    end
    ssh_keys.each_with_index do |entry, i|
      unless entry.is_a?(Hash) && entry["key"].present?
        errors.add(:ssh_keys, "entry #{i + 1} must have a key")
        next
